title: leontodon taraxacum var. obliquus
intro: Leontodon taraxacum var. obliquus, also known as the dandelion, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, making it a popular remedy for digestive issues and liver health. The plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals such as potassium and iron, providing numerous health benefits, including supporting immune function, reducing blood pressure, and promoting healthy digestion. Leontodon taraxacum var. obliquus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used as a food ingredient in salads and as a coffee substitute, making it a versatile and useful herb.
